metadata.title: Research Radioligand Theranostics: PSMA-617 and the Image-Then-Treat Loop

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: What clicked is the image-then-treat loop — Ga-68 selects, Lu-177 treats, post-therapy imaging confirms, and it can escalate to Ac-225. I want to compare the two validated pairs. Can you (1) put PSMA-617 and DOTATATE side by side on target, isotope, trial, and survival benefit; (2) explain what having two working pairs teaches about what makes a target theranostic-ready; and (3) reason about whether VISION's four-month overall-survival gain is clinically decisive?

props.command: What clicked is the image-then-treat loop — Ga-68 selects, Lu-177 treats, post-therapy imaging confirms, and it can escalate to Ac-225. I want to compare the two validated pairs. Can you (1) put PSMA-617 and DOTATATE side by side on target, isotope, trial, and survival benefit; (2) explain what having two working pairs teaches about what makes a target theranostic-ready; and (3) reason about whether VISION's four-month overall-survival gain is clinically decisive?
props.segment: Research Radioligand Theranostics: PSMA-617 and the

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Image it. Treat it. Image it again.

lines: Image it. Treat it. Image it again.

beats.narration_text: The same molecule can be used to image a cancer and to treat it — just swap the radioisotope. Gallium-68-PSMA-617 shows you every prostate cancer metastasis. Lutetium-177-PSMA-617 kills them. Treat only what you can see. This is what rational cancer therapy looks like: dose the patient only when the target is confirmed, and confirm efficacy by imaging again after.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — title card: PSMA THERANOSTICS: IMAGE-THEN-TREAT; Ga-68 (imaging) vs Lu-177 (therapy) paired on same PSMA-617 scaffold; VISION trial result summary

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to research the PSMA theranostic pair — the target, the imaging mechanism, the VISION trial, and alpha versus beta emitter comparison.

props.command:
claude "Research the PSMA radioligand theranostic pair in
prostate cancer. Explain: (1) why PSMA is an ideal
target (overexpression, functional folate transporter,
membrane-facing domain); (2) Ga-68-PSMA-617 PET
imaging mechanism; (3) Lu-177-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto)
therapy — VISION trial design and OS result; (4)
alpha vs. beta emitters — Ac-225 (alpha) shorter range,
higher LET vs Lu-177 (beta); (5) off-target uptake
organs as dose-limiting toxicity."

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) — Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code before trusting it. The script builds the theranostic pair table — isotope, application, range, LET, trial name, and OS benefit. The VISION trial result is on one line. Verify the numbers before the claim stands.

props.code:
# psma_theranostics.py — Claude Code output
PAIR = [
  {"isotope": "Ga-68",  "use": "PET IMAGING",
   "range": "~1 mm",   "let": "low",
   "trial": "diagnostic only"},
  {"isotope": "Lu-177", "use": "THERAPY (Pluvicto)",
   "range": "1-2 mm",  "let": "beta",
   "trial": "VISION (NEJM 2021): OS 15.3 vs 11.3 mo"},
  {"isotope": "Ac-225", "use": "THERAPY (next-gen)",
   "range": "~50 µm", "let": "alpha (high)",
   "trial": "Phase I/II ongoing"},
]
for p in PAIR:
    print(f"{p['isotope']}: {p['use']} | range={p['range']} | {p['trial']}")

beats.role_note: OPEN vox slot → Manim video B04_TheranosticLoop
beats.narration_text: Run it. The four-box loop: Ga-68 PSMA PET imaging selects eligible patients, Lu-177 therapy treats them, post-therapy imaging assesses the response. If response is confirmed, the loop repeats. If not, it can escalate to Ac-225. The VISION trial result sits below the loop — lutetium-177 improved overall survival by four months in PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_TheranosticLoop: 4-box loop IMAGE → SELECT → TREAT → ASSESS → back to IMAGE; return arrow in GOLD with escalation note; VISION trial result annotation below

beats.narration_text: The PSMA pair is not the only theranostic pair. Now iterate: ask Claude to compare the PSMA theranostic pair with the DOTATATE pair in neuroendocrine tumors — and what having two validated pairs teaches us.

props.command:
claude "Compare the PSMA pair (Ga-68/Lu-177-PSMA-617)
with the DOTATATE pair in neuroendocrine tumors.
What structural similarities exist? What clinical
evidence parallels the VISION trial? What does having
two validated theranostic pairs teach us about which
tumor types are candidates for this approach?"

beats.narration_text: The DOTATATE pair mirrors the PSMA pair exactly: Ga-68-DOTATATE images somatostatin-receptor-positive neuroendocrine tumors; Lu-177-DOTATATE (Lutathera) treats them. The NETTER-1 trial showed the same loop principle at work — image to confirm target, treat, image to confirm response. Two validated pairs define the rule: the loop works when the target is quantifiable by the same ligand scaffold.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — comparison table: PSMA pair (prostate cancer) vs DOTATATE pair (neuroendocrine); same loop structure, different target; shared requirements: quantifiable imaging, membrane-facing target, selective biodistribution

beats.narration_text: So theranostics is the explicit integration of precision oncology and targeted therapy. You do not treat unless you can confirm the target is there. The image-then-treat loop turns imaging from a diagnostic tool into a treatment eligibility filter — and potentially a response monitoring tool in the same patient visit.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — summary card: THERANOSTIC RULE: confirm target → treat → confirm response; loop vs. line model of cancer treatment; VISION trial as the proof of concept

beats.narration_text: Your move: check whether the tumor you are studying has a membrane-facing target that is overexpressed on cancer and low in dose-limiting normal tissue. That is the requirement for a theranostic pair. Then check whether there is an imaging isotope on the same scaffold. If both are true, the theranostic loop is possible.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — next steps card: 1) identify membrane-facing overexpressed target; 2) verify low normal tissue expression (salivary glands and kidneys as PSMA off-target example); 3) check for imaging isotope on same scaffold

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. So theranostics is the explicit integration of precision oncology and targeted therapy. You do not treat unless you can confirm the target is there. The image-then-treat loop turns imaging from a diagnostic tool into a treatment eligibility filter — and potentially a response monitoring tool in the same patient visit. Your move: check whether the tumor you are studying has a membrane-facing target that is overexpressed on cancer and low in dose-limiting normal tissue. That is the requirement for a theranostic pair. Then check whether there is an imaging isotope on the same scaffold. If both are true, the theranostic loop is possible.

beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.
